rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iodo-trifluoroethoxylation of terminal alkenes using an aryl iodide as iodine source and TFE as nucleophile/solvent
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the aryl iodide (2-iodo-1,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzene) generates an electrophilic/radical iodine species that adds across the alkene; TFE then traps the resulting cation/intermediate

key_intermediates.name: aryl iodide-derived I-centered radical or iodonium
β-iodo carbon radical or iodiranium ion from alkene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the electron-rich aryl iodide to release an electrophilic iodine equivalent that adds to the alkene; TFE acts as the oxygen nucleophile to capture the resulting cationic/radical intermediate and deliver the β-iodo trifluoroethyl ether.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrolysis with 2.5 equiv BHT, yield of 3a dropped to 19% and 73% of alkene 2a was recovered, consistent with a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: N-iodosuccinimide (NIS)
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing the aryl iodide with NIS under non-electrochemical conditions (DCM/TFE, rt, 25 h, no current) gave 3a in 58% yield (2:1), supporting that an electrophilic I+ source can drive the iodo-etherification independent of electricity

notes: The NIS control suggests an I+ / iodiranium (ionic) pathway is viable, while BHT suppression supports a radical component — consistent with a radical/polar crossover mechanism in which anodic oxidation of ArI generates an iodine radical that adds to the alkene, followed by oxidation to a cation trapped by TFE.
